page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The server is equipped with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It does not include any graphics processing units.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing placing in the top 25% at 97228.89. Single-core performance is more moderate, with Geekbench single-core scores categorized as weak. The system demonstrates strong memory bandwidth for cached small blocks at 3791.56 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read and write speeds remain average. In application-specific tests, the server exhibits strong capabilities in software compilation with clang, ray tracing, static web serving throughput at 46.63 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 73.41 million operations per second. Conversely, single-threaded decompression and large-model LLM text generation show weaker relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gd.metal instance leverages the ARM64 architecture to deliver high multi-core compute density and local NVMe storage without the overhead of virtualization. This hardware profile is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, and database operations that benefit from fast local storage. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for static web serving and Redis operations, though single-core and LLM text generation performance are relatively weak. This server is designed for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations requiring fast local storage.

tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server combining 64 dedicated cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage for multi-threaded and high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz.
128.0 GB DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high-throughput static web serving.
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for efficient data transfer.
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations.
